commit.message:
Fix a crash with untransforming into lich form

Transforming out of lich form with eg. a flux bauble, casting death's door,
then ending transformation to return to lich form crashed the game because
the untransform wouldn't complete successfully. Additionally, death's door
could be maintained in vampire form (which forbids casting it) using this
same method. For consistency and to fix the crash, entering either undead
form while in death's door now ends the death's door status. This will
prompt for normal transformation; probably a prompt should be added for
untransform but I was unsure where to put it.

Closes #5277

Fix some false freezing cloud warnings. (#5264)

Noticed that even when worshipping Qazlal, freezing cloud still warns
when casting it on yourself.
Here the problem is two-fold:
* Check on cloud immunity was missing from lambda, could be useful for
monsters too
* Lambda was never called for player.

It seems like `include_player` is true only for freezing cloud check so
it should not affect anything else.

commit.message:
Fix a startup crash with force_ability_targeter

Attempting to get the name of this ability without having previously used
Exegesis tried to pull up the name of SPELL_NO_SPELL, which asserts (and
this option seemingly results in trying to get the name of all abilities
at startup)

I'm not fully certain if spell_title(SPELL_NO_SPELL) should assert rather
than just return something like "No Spell", but for now I'm fixing this at
the level of Repeat Exegesis itself.

Allow using Imprint Weapon / Imbue Servitor if spell is usable via Exegesis

These two abilities are normally only usable if the player has memorized
Platinum Paragon / Spellspark Servitor. But they are good Exegesis targets,
which created the awkward situation where in order to cast Paragon via
Exegesis at all, you had to first memorize it (with potentially 100%
failure rate), imprint *something* on it, and then likely forget the spell
again.

Instead, make these abilities available to the player if the spell is in
their library and Exegesis is usable. (I had worried this might be slightly
confusing to the player, but it's plausibly less confusing than the way
things currently work).

Fix elementalist ancestors casting LRD on the player for real damage (Lici)

Damage done to the focal point of LRD (ie: if casting it directly on a
creature) wasn't handled via beam code and thus bypassed the protection the
player gets from their own ancestor. However, the tracer also isn't aware
of this, and thinks the player cannot be harmed.

As a result, ancestors would freely deconstruct susceptible players in
order to hit enemies.

It feels a little incoherant to allow them to blow you up and it *not* do
any damage to you, so now all LRD casters are entirely prevented from
directly targeting a creature that is immune to all their effects.

Fix backspace sometimes not working on macOS (perryprog)

Some terminals send keycode 127 (ASCII DEL) when you press the backspace
key. We have special handling for this but were accidentally checking for
keycode -127

Fix a crash-on-demand in some webtiles versions, remove a menu param

When isspace() was called in prompt_invent_item() on non-character keycodes
(such as numpad arrows), it could result in a crash. The function
apparently has undefined behavior for negative numbers which may be
locale-specific (it results in a crash on certain servers and not others,
for instance).

I am not certain what the 'correct' solution is to calling this function on
this sort of keyboard input. Fortunately, it looks like the code in
question is essentially superfluous: spaces already exit almost all menus
at an earlier point in this codepath. The only menus it *doesn't* are
god weapon blessings and Ash cursing/uncursing items. And even in those
cases, it seems to essentially only result in not printing 'Huh?' when you
press space (after already having pressing a letter of an invalid item),
unlike any other invalid input.

I can see no reason these menus need to behave in this narrowly different
way and am simply removing this behavior.

This fixes #5273.
update credits

commit.message: fix another docs typo

Greatly increase exploration piety gained in Ziggurats

While it is fine (perhaps even desirable) if exploration and kill piety
have slightly different dynamics between various parts of the game, they're
broadly comparable for a majority of it. Zigs, however, have extreme enemy
density compared to the rest of the game. While there's some variance
involved, a mid-speed exploration god earns something like 1/5th the piety
of a standard kill god on their first Zig. Even if this makes some measure
of sense (there's lots of things to kill and not much to explore), I feel
it makes certain gods needlessly poor compared to other gods.

In the interest of more equality, this commit greatly boosts exploration
piety in Zigs. Since Zig maps actually can vary a great deal in size,
depending on which generator a Zig chooses to use, this is also normalized
by the same function Zig generators use to determine map area. The end
result should (very approximately) triple the current piety these gods
receive in Zigs.

Kill gods will still have superior piety income, even in a first zig (and
the gap will widen considerably when megazigging), but this should narrow
the gap enough to make a wider variety of gods able to keep up with Zig
threats while spending their piety actively.

Fix battlespheres sticking to melee range of enemies (qoalabear)

Battlesphere technically don't have spells, which made their threat_range
appear to be 0 since 7ec163d resulting in their M_MAINTAIN_RANGE behavior
no longer working.

Experimentally adjust Knight ancestors

These changes are motivated by several factors:
 -Knights are the plainest design of the 3 ancestor types and have the
  least exciting capstone upgrade (gaining ~22% increased melee damage on
  the ancestor who had by far the lowest melee damage until battlemage was
  replaced)
 -An attempt to lean futher into emphasising their role as a 'tank' by
  offering unique utility to assist with that role (in the same way that
  elementalists lean further away from generalists than battlemages did).
 -(With elementalists no longer gaining haste, certain other devs felt it
  was more odd for 2 of 3 to have it instead of each spell being unique to
  a given ancestor)

Knight ancestors no longer gain Haste at XL 19, but instead gain Bolster
(a similar self-buff that instead grants Might+Resistance). This gives a
little more defensive power in lategame, while providing a similar
offensive boost as before (and leaving the true speed increase to Hexers).

More uniquely, at XL 15 they gain the ability to briefly Bind adjacent
enemies in place with their melee attacks. This is a very short-duration
effect, but can be applied with a high chance - effectively allowing a
knight to 'obstruct' a wider space and catch enemies trying to slip past
them towards a player who might be hiding behind them.

Since it is dependant on their attack speed (and knights attack slowly), it
is less reliable when first gained and improves meaningfully when they gain
speed brand at XL 24 (improving the impact of that upgrade).

(Incidentally, this ability was originally designed to *be* a new XL 24
capstone ability, but playing with it, I felt that it created enough
interesting situations that it merited being tried as a core part of the
knight's kit from much earlier.)

Finally, knights also gain a 25% HP increase at XL 24 to feel like their
final upgrade has a bit more parity with the other two ancestors. (Also:
ancestor durability suffers somewhat in endgame and knights provide less
impact in other ways.)

I am a bit conscious that this is *mostly* a buff to a popular ancestor of
an already strong god, but it felt fun to me and I expect this should have
no more than a modest effect in the part of the game where Hep's power is
strongest.

In any case, let's see how other people find it.

(Bolster spell icon made by regret-index, combining ontoclasm's resistance
and might potion and status icons with CanofWorm's mutation resistance
icons.)

Allow monsters bound in place to attack other nearby targets

If a monster was Bound and attempted to move towards its primary target, it
would pass its turn instead. This meant that monsters bound in place next
to player allies would often just sit there and let themselves be hit
repeatedly until the status wore off.

Now they will attempt to melee some valid enemy in range, rather than pass
the turn.

(Getting spellcasters to consider casting a *spell* at some target in range
is a wildly more difficult task, but this should still be a good status
quo improvement in most situations.)

Replace Battlemage ancestors with Elementalists

While Hep battlemages may not have been 'weak' by some objective measure,
they suffered from a couple of issues:

Firstly, a recurring player complaint was that, despite being a ranged
attacker, they would repeatedly fling themselves headlong into enemies and
die rather than cast spells. However, any AI adjustments to keep them at
range would have been awkward given that their melee damage was (by design)
also considerable.

Secondly (and more subtly), despite having very different spells from
Hexer, the sort of character who would want to choose battlemage and hexer
were often nearly the same (and hexer has widely come to be seen as the
superior of the two choices - in both power and popularity).

So this is an attempt to further differentiate them by reimagining
battlemage as a more fragile ranged attacker - one whose damage output is
more consistent, but requires more effort from the player character to
protect them.

Elementalists have reduced HP compared to other ancestors, and their melee
attack is very poor, but they are cautious (and thus less likely to
approach enemies) and have higher cast rates of longer-range spells.
They have the following spells and abilities:

XL 1: Shock, Stone Arrow, Deflect Missiles
XL 14: Shock->Iceblast, Stone Arrow->Bolt of Magma
XL 19: LRD (as a 3rd attack spell), plus globally increased spell damage
XL 24: Iceblast->Permafrost Eruption, Bolt of Magma->Plasma Beam

(It's worth noting that while Deflect Missiles is helpful to the ancestor,
it's also somewhat unhelpful to the *player* as this means that the
ancestor generally cannot defend you from ranged attacks at all - they'll
just pass through and hit you instead - reinforcing their role as a pure
attacker).

This ancestor provides high damage output, but their fragility and lack of
melee ability means that the player character must be confident in their
ability to keep them alive. (Also, they make a lot of noise starting at
XL 15, which is a meaningful downside to be aware of.) The hope is that
this feels more distinct in playstyle from Hexers (giving more nuance to
the choice of ancestor type), as well as somewhat less frustrating to play
than battlemage could be. Also, they get to use some more modern and AoE
spells (battlemage's current spell list was designed when they still did
friendly fire to the player, and thus explosion spells wouldn't have been
very effective on them).
Add a couple of mages from more recent spells to randbooks.txt

Make a smaller percentage of Sif book gifts carry Sif's name

It's a little monotonous and what library worth its salt isn't filled with
tomes by *different* authors?

Weight Sif books to the player's skill a little less

With it being a little easier for Sif worshippers to utilize spells
outside their core skills, it feels appropriate to me for Sif to be a
little more diverse in their gifting at the same time.

Make Sif abilities care about Spellcasting instead of Invocations

Exegesis is one of Sif's most unique and flashy benefits, but even with it
being a bit more flexible and affordable, the investment required to get it
online can compete directly with actually being able to cast the spells
you're gifted yourself. By borrowing a page from Kiku's book, and instead
scaling off spellcasting skill, it is easier to both improve as a mage
*and* have access to Sif's neat tricks at the same time. Additionally,
encouraging the player to prioritize spellcasting (rather than school
skills) more than they otherwise would means they're also in a better
position to make use of more of Sif's varied gifts.

The skill investment required to get Exegesis online is raised a little in
compensation (and the scaling of Channel Energy reduced slightly), but it
should nonetheless be accessible earlier without sacrificing magic skills.
I expect this to be a buff to the god, but Sif has been somewhat
underperforming for a while and I also think this potentially makes Sif
gameplay more *interesting* while being very flavorful.

Adjust Sif piety mechanics

Expressing devotation to the Loreminder primarily via killing one's enemies
in their name has always felt a little bit at odds with Sif's flavour (and
is something repeatedly brought up by players as feeling a little off).

Since Sif piety dynamics are already being shook up by other system changes
at the moment, this feels like a good time to try switching them to
exploration piety instead - which is also a well-tested and inobtrusive
system. It also does a little more to differentiate them from Vehumet in
terms of mechanical flavour (instead of, for instance, both gods
effectively having a direct way to turn kills into MP).

I've also made the rate at which one is gifted books no longer scale with
piety; if the player is over the threshold where gifts can be given, the
rate will always be the same. This is to avoid discouraging the player from
using their abilities (especially Exegesis) in order to get gifts more
quickly.

Repurpose name generation from level-based randbooks for other randbooks

'Level-based' names can now be used for any randbook whose spells all fit
into one of a few specific level ranges (1, 2-4, 4-6, or 7-9), and
school-based descriptors can be slotted into them in place of the 'generic'
magecraft descriptors previously used for level-based books. A few of these
combinations sound a bit... odd, but most of them work (and amusingly weird
titles is part of the appeal of randbook names in the first place, in my
opinion).

In addition, the 'generic' magecraft descriptors previously used here can
now be used for any randbook whose content is so mixed that at least 3
different schools are tied for the most common.

Remove fixed-level randbooks

Randart spellbooks came in two main varieties: ones themed around spell
schools, and ones whose spells all shared the same level. The latter were
far rarer (accounting for only ~1.5% of spellbook acquirements - 1/30th as
common as the other type) and the code involved was strange in several
ways.

For example:
 -Even when generated by acquirement, there was no weighting towards spell
  schools the player knew
 -High level spells require such high item level that it was impossible to
  attain them for almost all of the game
 -Said high level spellbooks would have only contained a single spell in
  most cases anyway
 -These books tried to avoid containing spells *the player currently has
  memorized*, even when not generated by acquirement!

The latter case matters less these days, since such books have mostly not
generated as part of floor loot since 99ad798, but one vault still used
them. It is extremely against policy for something to work this way (and
allowed the player to slightly influence floor loot by, say, memorizing
useless spells to reduce the chances of being offered them in randbooks.
Probably not to a great enough degree to be *worth* it, but bad policy
nonetheless.)

Instead of trying to fix its various problems (and rewrite its
oddly-structured code), I am opting to remove this randbook type entirely.
'Unrelated spells of one level' isn't an especially compelling design, they
already barely existed, and this slightly reduces the number of disjoint
codepaths that can create randart books (there are still 3 more of them!)

A bunch of options for randbook naming were used only for this category of
randbook, which I will attempt to repurpose in a subsequent commit.

Allow repeating spells with Divine Exegesis at reduced piety cost

Divine Exegesis is a unique and flexible ability in theory, but its high
piety cost (and often somewhat low power) tends to bias its usage towards
spells that have a strong effect with a single cast (such as Revivification
or Dragon's Call) and away from those intended to be cast many times in
sequence (like a lot of blasting spells).

This attempts to broaden its usage a little by allowing the player to
repeat the last spell they recently cast via Exegesis at a greatly reduced
piety cost (currently 1/4 of Exegesis itself). This should make it much
more cost-effective to use a variety of spells that would not have been
appealing prior to this change.

(This recasting is done via a new ability that is available for a short
time after using Exegesis itself.)

Tweak Brothers in Arms

BiA's code had not been updated in well over a decade, and had some mildly
awkward properties. It treated two-headed ogres as weaker spawns than
plain trolls (since it predated the damage buff two-headed ogres recieved
when hill giants were removed), and the power calculations involved meant
that the most of the chance to get stone giants (the best result) depended
on piety being meaningfully *above* 6* (one had a ~5% chance when first
reaching 6* piety, but a 40% chance at 200 piety).

These days, we try to actively minimize power boosts gotten 'above 6*' and
this is a significant one. Also, prior to recent piety changes, decay and
gift timeouts made it hard to maintain high summon quality, even if one
rarely used the ability, but that should no longer be true. And while
giving Trog a buff is intentional, I want to apply a little more control to
the effect.

The quality of summons from BiA is now based on your XL rather than piety.
The list is better-ordered in terms of power, and several new monsters have
been added to it (drawing upon ones already used by hostile spriggan
berserkers, and a few others). It should be more consistent about not
allowing extreme low-rolls (though stone giants only become possible by
midgame).

The piety cost is increased mildly (though this should still be effectively
cheaper than it used to be) and the failure rate should no longer be quite
so high when the ability is first gained.

(Mostly) eliminate the effect of god gifts on piety

Receiving gifts from Trog, Sif, and Vehumet, as well as ammo gifts from
Oka, caused a piety timeout that nullified 75% of all piety gains until it
was paid off. This had a few awkward effects, most prominantly with Sif,
where it (combined with piety decay and the normal stepdowns at high piety)
made it very difficult to ever reach 6* piety until all spells were known
(so gifts would cease). Being unable to champion Sif for the tournament
banner has been a recurring complaint for years.

But also, this system was pretty inflexible. While it had a major effect on
available piety for these gods, the only way to modulate it was to make
gifts less common - which in fact was done *twice* for Sif (61718b2 and
50735af) without actually fully achieving the stated goals (and potentially
slightly weakening them at the same time). It seems healthier to me to more
heavily decouple gifts and piety, so that either can be independently
adjusted as we see fit.

This commit removes the piety cost for the gift timeout from Sif, Okawaru,
and Trog. It remains for Vehumet only, since they are a fairly unique case
where it was used *only* to control gifting schedule (there is no way to
spend piety, and no passives gained or lost at the last several piety
tiers) and it seemed unnecessary to disrupt that directly at this time.

As a consequence of this change, piety gain for these 3 gods would be
significantly increased by mid-5*. While both Sif and Trog could arguably
handle this buff, Okawaru already feels in a relatively good place.
Moreover, they have the unique property among these gods of caring both
about how quickly you can first hit 6* (for the weapon/armour gift) *and*
how much piety you have to regularly spend on frequently-used abilities
afterward. After unsuccessfully experimenting with various ways to address
this without either making 6* much slower to reach *or* giving
wildly more piety to use on abilities (or sometimes both), I have
compromised by making Okawaru kills give gradually decreasing piety when
above 5*. The aim is to approximate the current behavior somewhat
reasonably (and even if it may seem awkward to replace one stepdown with
another, the new one is disentangled from any future gifting changes *and*
much easier to reason about, so I think it's still an improvement).

Sif and Trog will be getting a few more tweaks as a follow-up to this in a
subsequent commit.

Remove some code preventing djinni from getting Sif/Veh/Kiku gifts

Unnecessary since they can't worship these gods at all (and seemingly only
ever could for approximately one day in trunk).

Remove a mildly confusing ancient comment

I don't think there's any way in which they're more special than any other
type of kill, in terms of blame, for a very long time.

Make Okawaru piety a bit less random.

Removes some fuzzing from Okawaru piety gain, making the gain X/Y rather than
2dX/Y, before random rounding.

The randomisation behaviour was inconsistent with all other gods, who randomise
only to round to an integer, and meant that occasionally Okawaru would not
accept a perfectly cromulent kill (i.e. would not print a message for it).

Make sure we excommunicate on piety 0

Adds handling of excommunication for low piety to the function which
handles piety loss.

We previously removed this from the function which handled piety decay
decay, but this meant it was possible to reach 0 piety without
triggering excommunication (in one extremely unlikely way).

This has almost no practical implication vs handling it on a timer -
it's quite hard to get excommunicated for low piety other than via
penance, which was already calling this. But if the player manages to
reach 0 piety by removing an amulet of faith (quite a feat), they will
now be excommunicated immediately rather than in 1-10 turns.

Remove piety hysteresis

This mechanic was to support piety decay, and is no longer neeeded.

[Committer's note: Fixed some piety logging being accidentally
removed]

Fix TSO piety bug and rebalance

This fixes a bug where TSO got full piety for seeing evil creatures,
when it was intended they got none. This made their piety gain in evil
branches extremely high.

At the same time as adjusting gain rates to account for the removal of
piety decay, we fix this bug, and proportionally increase their other
sources of piety gain so that in a three-runer their piety is largely
unchanged. In extended, this is a significant nerf, but TSO still gets
60% more piety than other gods in fully evil locations.

A single playthrough suggests this roughly works; piety milestones
were hit at the same times within 10%, which is within random
variation.

[Committer's note: Fixed a couple math errors (hopefully).]

Remove piety decay

Remove piety decay, and rebalance piety gain for gods other than TSO
with the intention of keeping net piety gain rates fairly constant in
3-rune games. We leave TSO to a future commit because it is a
complicated special case.

Overall, we aim for the net piety rate to change by less than 10% for
all gods, piety ranks and reasonable playstyles, with the change
usually being far less than this. It will necessarily not be entirely
neutral - piety decay hurts slow characters more than fast ones.

To outline considerations and the methodology:
- We use piety logging to determine how much piety is gained vs lost
for various gods.
- We adjust piety gain accordingly so that when stepdowns are not in
play net piety gain is unaffected.
- If we stopped there, stepdowns would mean that at higher piety we
now gain faster, as decay has a bigger effect when gain is slower. So
we adjust the stepdowns to roughly correct this effect (though the
adjustment needed is different per god, so this will mean some gods
piety is faster or slower at high ranks, by up to around 10% at six
stars).
- We make (tiny) adjustments to killing piety to account for the
removal of holiness dependence.

For detailed analysis see
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1G7-EvOMX8yOKw2EuyDPSm6fUAaNl8dUXhWRwGc5Pp3c/edit?usp=sharing.

[Committer's note: Fixed a couple small numerical issues (hopefully)]

Simplify piety gain

The goal of this is to simplify the code and parameterisation of piety
gain.

We replace the complicated previous formula for gaining piety based on
an kills with:
- A base amount of gain, and
- A scaling factor based on the relative level of the monster and the
player.

This simplifies the previous system in the following ways:
- The holiness of the monster doesn't matter.
- The base gain and the scaling factor are disentangled; we can easily
change the piety gain of an event without changing how it scales with
monster level
- All piety gain from monsters scales with monster level in the same
way. Previously, kills for good gods and certain other niche kills did
not scale, but this seems an unnecessary extra piece of complexity.

There are some balance changes involved - while these aren't big,
the intent is to balance this when removing piety decay and testing
this all together.

Exploration piety does not change at all, but is refactored slightly
so that the denominator is 10,000 (making maths easier) and set
in the piety code.

Do not allow firing inner flame at monsters the player can't harm. (#5258)

This was always a useless cast ("nothing happens" or "X avoids your
attack"). Now we explain this in the targetter and do not allow
targetting.

commit.message:
Prohibit Okawaru from gifting orbs.

Also fixes a bug where a max shield skill spriggan could crash in acquirement.

Add wizmode command for resetting god capstones

Seems useful for testing.

Fix floors sometimes looking like walls (particleface, regret-⸸nde※)

Level generation sometimes leaves floors with feat flavour set to a wall
tile. Before commit a371595 this would have no effect on the tile used
by the floor as we special cased floors not to use it. However, this
special handling for floors was removed in that commit as I thought it
was part of a hack used to handle temporary terrain (other feature types
were excluded from using feat flavour as part of this hack).

More Gulch small fix-ups

Some debugging messages removed, and a mildly ugly way to prevent layouts
(currently only chambers_river) from placing mould patches underneath
stacks of scrap if the header isn't in the right order (as is poorly
communicated and plays badly for console).

Yet further Gulch updates

After four weeks of public testing, Gulch's killstats are still lagging
a fair bit behind the other post-Lair-branch threat portals, which
considering the additional threat levels of novel threats for the
unspoiled, is fairly lowered than ideal for such a portal's position.
It doesn't need to be hyper-lethal- Desolation already serves as a portal
with comparable unique enemies and higher rewards- but it can do with
some more consistent threat increases beyond the variations of maps and
traps and rare additional bosses. This commit covers both tweaks to
monsters and rewards alike:

 * Gulch monsters have been buffed mostly across their HP / AC / EV / cast
   rates / melee damage according to their killstats over the past month.
   This means significant buffs for mongrel wurms, fungal shamblers, and
   scrapshell chimeras, and fairly mild buffs for rusted inspectors,
   roaming sludgefish, and sewage sovereigns.

 * With some randomization and syntax fix-ups, there is no longer a ~1/7
   chance for a Gulch to get up to extra five item rewards- with the last
   rewards push in dd551d8, the item quality is high enough that I'm
   not willing to canonize the bug that caused this.

 * The mutation catalyst highlight floor tile has been slightly brightened
   up further, to match it a bit closer to the brightness of the normal
   autopickup outlines when wall shadows can cover some of it. The
   description and discovery message both also have further colour
   highlighting by default, as more quick additional cues towards paying
   any attention to them while more interactable features and some
   examination bugs are still being worked upon.

Buff some synergy mutations (DracoOmega)

Making these conditional-effect mutations actually worth diverting into
involves them having better upsides, and both of these mutations are
fairly low-rolls from mutation catalysts as were added in b14e334.

 * The spark swarm mutation (2550bce, applies the light-outline debuff
   when you inflict fire or electric damage) has its chance move from
   33%/66% to 40%/80% per hit. As a rather minor upside, it might as well
   be reasonably likely to actually apply. It is also a bit more common,
   as we've got more common and flashier mutations already.

 * The time-warped blood mutation (a98c639, grants haste at low health
   to highest-hd allies) now activates at 66% health, up from the prior
   50%. With more HP threshold effects in the game than previously, these
   are notably a bit awkward since one usually wants to flee at low health
   rather than fight with hasted allies- letting it activate at a somewhat
   higher threshold makes it far more of an interesting decision point.

Many new tiles + adjacent vault review

 * As a fairly delayed follow-up to cc690c2 and c880663, I have completed
   the rest of the player paper doll tiles for all randart melee weapons,
   each matching the palettes and loose shapes of those randarts as floor
   items, each using roctavian and ontoclasm's paper doll tiles for
   non-mundane melee weapons. This should help with visual clarity in
   immediately conveying monsters wielding randarts as frequently comes
   with random resists, and should have fairly higher consistency with
   the small number of weapons that previously had randart weapon tiles
   like eveningstars or lajatangs.

   * (This doesn't yet come with updates for ranged weapons lagging even
      further behind in displaying egos at all, much less randarts,
      as it's a fair bit more work to handle centaurs and yaktaurs having
      completely bespoke tiles for their posing. Further updates for such
      will come at some point.)

    * (This also comes with some file renames, as I really didn't want
       to continue the legacy of quick blades using dagger.png or
       bardiches using e.g. glaive_three2.png.)

 * Permafrost Eruption now has unique vfx entirely, made from Denzi's
   old explosion and LRD icon graphics, to both differentiate from other
   cold spells in ignoring AC as well as granting the center tile of the
   effect use a different graphic to emphasize that spot as hit twice.

 * Lom Lobon's floor now has unique floor tiles instead of reusing generic
   recoloured ones, heavily editing a mix of Denzi's crystal floor squares
   with the CC0 diamond patterns from TextureNinja used currently in some
   Zot vaults, to try to hew closer to the organized place of study that
   Lom's flavour suggests.

 * The floor_etched tileset of 38c1deb has almost never been used in the
   thirteen years since it was added, as its brightness and palette are
   rather distractingly visible and mesmerizing. To try to salvage it into
   something more broadly usable this much later, I've heavily edited it
   into faint rainbow glows on dark black, which should still stand out
   versus other floors while functioning better as a tile in Crawl itself.
   This has been thus deployed to ten or so statistically-lethal and
   highly generic Depths vaults, as another small attempt to make the
   bespoke flashiness of vaults actually be more easily memorable between
   one another, as well as within Ziggurats past the first cleared one, to
   use less generic tiles within those as well as to tie them a little
   closer to the branch the entrance is guaranteed to place in.

 * Spriggan druids now have an edited tile without a staff for when they
   lose their quarterstaff to Tukima's Dance.

Correctly match up some lore dragon names (Ge0FF)

Being consistent with unpronounceable names is hard.

Fix floors under features sometimes not using LFLOORTILE (regret-⸸nde※)

When a vault placed a feature with a custom tile but didn't set a custom
tile for the floor under the feature, the floor under the feature would
use the default tile for floors in the branch even if the vault had its
own default floor tile using LFLOORTILE. This is because the default
floor tile would be set to the one for the branch and then we would
apply custom tiles to features which called tile_init_flavour to choose
the tile varient but had the unintended side effect of setting the floor
tile to the default floor tile if it hadn't been set yet, we would then
set the default floor tile to the one defined by the vault using
LFLOORTILE and then we would call tile_init_flavour for every square to
set its floor tile to the default floor tile if it hadn't been set yet.

To fix this, we can remove the call to tile_init_flavour from where we
apply custom tiles to features as the tile varient will be choosen when
we call tile_init_flavour on every square anyway.

Don't claim that teleports cancel Polar Vortex (Ver)

They haven't since 1e625b3 but I hadn't realized that this behavior was
even mentioned in-game at the time.

Fix boulder damage always being attributed to the player (various)

Among other things, this meant that if a hostile player ghost rolled a
boulder into one of your apostles, you would get penance for it. Whoops.

Remove some unused targeter code

Neither used nor needed since e713047

Fix aiming Fulsome Fusilade via quiver showing confusing beam paths (Odds)

Fulsome Fusilade was receiving a default targeter_beam since it defined a
zap. But until e713047 this had been basically invisible. However, changes
to how target are refined by the interface resulted in drawing an apparant
beam path to a random enemy (usually). This is due to how only spells with
target flags set their aim based on cursor movement, allowing
find_acceptable_aim() to leave leftover state.

To fix this, no longer give a default targeter_beam to spells without any
target flags. Additionally, don't do target refinement in
find_acceptable_aim() for said untargetable spells. (This is superfluous in
this case and perhaps all of them, but I can't be certain of that and it
seems a reasonable safeguard.)

Fix a lua DES call

This should use KITEM, since it's defining a key glyph.

Fix floor under used mutation catalyst changing tile on reload

When you use a mutation catalyst, the floor tile under it is changed to
a variation of the gulch floor tile. However, because tile variations
are rechoosen on loading a game, the logic used to pick a variation must
be the same in both places or the tile might get a different variation
after reloading the game.

Fix used mutation catalysts using floor tile for unused catalyst

If you activated a mutation catalyst while playing in console and then
continued player the game in tiles, the floor under the used mutation
catalyst would show the tile for the under an unused catalyst.

Tweak some tension code for sleeping monsters

ef33468 both accidentally forgot to remove the case for reducing fleeing
and sleeping monsters being worth more tension as well an accidental
flip of the fraction those were worth. As a net result, this meant
asleep monsters were worth the same tension values as them being awake.
I don't really agree with the intent of old tension calculations nearly
completely tossing out the threat of any nearby sleeping monsters- there's
always a decent chance they'll wake up at any moment- so this commit
cleans up the code a little and semi-canonizes this to make them worth 66%
of their tension. It also tweaks some redundant lines for fleeing and
paralysed monsters.

Remove a redundant "Error:" in erroneous wizmode item creation

The "Error:" prefix for such item failures was made redundant with 02af3d5
making all failed item creation display an incorrect itemspec.

A scattered few new tiles

 * Bear simulacra have a new tile, combining ontoclasm's small and large
   quadruped simulacrum tiles, so that they're more distinguishable from
   wolf and elephant simulacra in early Ice Caves.

 * Purified mutation catalysts now have a unique floor tile underneath
   them meant to be a square glow resembling the autopick outline, which
   hopefully should help players actually notice them as features to
   interact with. (As with the emptied catalyst itself, this tile changes
   to a regular Gulch floor tile after its use). This tile still uses
   the normal Gulch tile sources of roctavian's adjustments of Denzi's V
   floor tiles with roctavian and ontoclasm's Slime floor tiles.
   (The catalyst tile has also been slightly nudged up and diminished in
   floor shadow so it can show the new outline better.)

 * Ynoxinuls' Ufetubi Swarm spell has a new icon to hopefully help with
   players noticing it got upgraded to summon berserk ufetubi in 6c75973.
   This tile combines ontoclasm's ufetubus tile and generic summoning
   spell icon sigil with Purge's old berserk status icon.

New mutation: fire or electric damage outlines foes in light

This commit introduces another good mutation meant to provide variety
for a category of passives heavily defined by generic stats, and follows
up on the parent commits of elemental checks by adding a much weaker
effect of multiple element types to the general mutation pool instead
of a single effect to the unrands pool. (The power level might be a little
too weak, but the current sources of it as an irresistable effect have
some popularity even when they come with other downsides, so this should
be fine as a minor upside.)

This rare two level mutation, flavoured as a subtle swarm of sparks and
cinders, gives a 33% / 66% chance according to its level to inflict the
softly glowing / outlined in light status when you deal non-zero fire
damage to enemies without rF+++ or electric damage to enemies without
rElec+++. This debuff, which is roughly a 10-15% increase in accuracy
in effect, is currently available to players via helmets of light or
TSO's halo without any chance check in exchange for recieving the same
penalty as well as halved stealth- without such downsides, going through
a different condition and chance "for free" seems like a passable
exchange. (Compared to Corona as was removed in cafe5c4 and d4a3b56, this
effect also is coming for free when actually making some progress against
killing a monster by actually hitting it, so it should feel less awful
than just rolling chances for a single weakling hex forever).

Aside from mildly helping fiery and electric magic / weapons /
evocables / consumables, it also counts as inflicting a debuff for athames
or the swamp witch's dragon scales, which should interweave it in enough
ways to make it useful despite the relatively low numbers. This commit
also adds an icon for the glowing effect again to make it noticeable when
it applies (as with the rest of these checks, there's too many ways to
do these effects enmass for it to get a message each time), and does some
minor hack work to get Vhi's Electric Charge working for it.

The mutation icon combines roctavian's old lightning scales tile,
ontoclasm's old electrocution brand and resist fire ego icons, Bloax's
Corona spell icon, and Sastreii's head-slot mutation icons. The new Corona
icon notably edits ontoclasm's summoned icon (rather than being nearly as
visible as ontoclasm's old icon for such).

New unrands: the fire dragon occultist scales + ice dragon arcanist scales

These unrands both follow up the elemental damage checks of the parent
commit and the caster-promoting Dungeon-mage-flavoured dragon scales of
2d801cb. They also expand a little on the only dragons with speech or
characterization being named uniques, get another few rare usages of the
Exposed debuff introduced in 962f017 for Gale Centaurs, and further
expand on the specific-spell-school wizardy effects introduced for various
talismans and heavier armour egos alike.

These fire dragon scales and ice dragon scales unrands are of a pair both
thematically (given backstories of originating from more hard-to-pronounce
ancient dragons of recently-opposite wizard types of 129f0b0), but also to
support their mechanical benefits having a narrowed focus that can
encourage pivoting into their specific needs. The fire dragon occultist
scales are +6 FDS of {FireExpos FireWiz rF++ rC- Int+3} and the ice dragon
arcanist scales are +7 IDS of {IceExpos IceWiz rC++ rF- Str+3}, with two
unique artefact properties specified for each of them:

 * FireExpos / IceExpos: This gives a 50% chance for non-zero fire & magma
   / cold and ice damage dealt by the wearer to inflict the Exposed
   status, which increases damage taken by non-weapon-attack sources by
   35%, makes non-weapon beams more accurate in hitting the bearer, and
   lowers their Willpower by 30. (Much like Fimbulwinter in c6d8f86 and
   the swamp witch dragon scales in 2d801cb, even partially-resistable
   damage can't inflict the status on those with the matching rF∞ or rC∞,
   and as with many debuff sources, this can't indefinitely extend the
   status with further applications of fire or cold damage. Implementation
   wise, this mostly only tweaks Ignite Poison to use BEAM_FIRE.)

   As with Fimbulwinter in the previous commit, this effect comes with
   interesting upsides for Makhleb and Qazlal invocations, various
   consumables like immolation and iceblast, and the corresponding spell
   schools (if one can cast in the scales at all). Unlike Fimbulwinter,
   this also works with any given flaming or freezing weapon one wants,
   though it won't help those weapons themselves do any more direct
   damage- such infinitely-available-swings can still help for hex wands
   or those other invocations and consumables, which should still be
   neat in-and-of-itself.

 * FireWiz / IceWiz: This gives the equivalent of one ring of wizardy
   (and not a second, like rimehorn / scarab / riddle talismans or
   heavy armour egos) for spells of the matching school. This is still
   somewhat offset by the actual encumbrance of the armour itself, but
   should still encourage alongside the Exposed access to actually
   cast specific destructive schools in mildly heavy armour, and can
   also encourage focusing on those schools when in their overlaps with
   e.g. Forgecraft or Ice or Earth.

While these are both very straightforward in a certain fashion by
essentially being fire and ice scales boosting fire and ice magic, Crawl
has shyed away from such condition-check paradigms of modern roguelike
genre-hybrid designs, and Crawl's eternal design thirst for more build
variety + unique drops may as well start basic iterations upon this
synergies space. (It is highly unlikely with these particularly restrained
drops of unrands or rarer other means to result in cascading hyper-combos
of various effects too complicated to parse, especially in contrast to
what one one can already do for weapon damage).

Their item tiles combine roctavian's old fire / ice dragon armour and
old salamander firebrand tile with Sastreii's current fire / ice dragon
armour and shattered gem tiles. The player doll tiles combine ontoclasm's
current fire and ice dragon scale paper doll tiles with Denzi's old
tiles for the same.

New unrand: the athame "Fimbulwinter"

As with e17ad08, it's nice for there to be unrands for each weapon base
type, with their inherent high competition in their slot as well as
providing a bit more unique play per weapon type per run. This athame
unrand both plays with the debuffs the base weapon and weapon type both
care about, experiments with Bane and Doom as hasn't touched any of the
unrand pool, and is the first to set up a slow trickle of experimenting
further with simple and broad conditional triggers beyond the debuff
system set-up by poltergeists and athames themselves.

This +9 freezing athame "Fimbulwinter" is named after the Norse myth of
a three-year winter preceding Ragnarok (tying as well as it can to Doom),
and is interlinked with Crawl's own cosmology via prophets of Qazlal
rather than shying away from mythology entirely as was done to Zephyr née
Sharnaga across 76effe1 and 0de3dc5 (...and which has come back full
circle with referring to Zephyrus with Gale Centaurs in 652d35c). On the
downsides property list, it comes with Bane and rF- to make it a full
investment comparable to ^Contam and ^Fragile unrands. These are meant
to compensate for both a thematic rC++ and a unique IceDoom property:
the wielder directly dealing any non-zero cold damage to enemies without
rC∞ (i.e. clouds but not allies) also gives them 5 + 4d3 Doom, which for
monsters activates at 50 Doom to provide instead of Banes 100 to 200
turns of irresistable slowing, weakness, vitrification, blinding, or
fairly heavy draining.

By being on an athame, this allows fairly strong direct melee in exchange
for a fairly different clause of racking up cold damage- while enough
swings at a foe can inflict statuses eventually, one can also deploy
damage-over-time spells like Frozen Ramparts or Freezing Cloud, which also
makes Metabolic Englaciation a little less of a troubled spell itself.
It also can augment Iceblast charges or the Passive Freeze mutation,
provide a bit more of an extra edge to lucky Qazlal and Makhleb
invocations, and grant a fairly different spell-booster to more focused
Ice Magic destructive spell mages, which should all be interesting side
notions of reward to compliment the innate risk of taking a random Bane.
(It can also start a precedent of tracking dealing damage types for
other future designs, to encourage anything other than stacking up
raw Slaying and spellpower.)

(Implementation-wise, this makes apply_damage_brand properly define an
attack flavour for most weapon brands instead of inexplicably just
electrocution, to make it easier to track these in the future. This should
have no mechanical effects aside from making flaming weapons capable of
burning down bushes. This commit also makes Doom befalling monsters
specify which of the very long debuffs is actually applied, since this
wasn't previously set up when it'd take charmed ravens or bribed orbs of
entropy to plausibly apply such without dealing massive damage first. It
doesn't admittedly ascribe it to Fimbulwinter or communicate in the message
log it lasts anything like its 100 to 200 turns- I'll see how people feel
about the in-game messaging here before doing more changes.)

The item tile combines roctavian's old plutonium sword tile and dagger of
Chilly Death tiles, while the paper doll tile once more modifies the
current quick blade paper doll tile also by roctavian.

Hopefully fix the build with gcc 4.7.2

One of the webtiles servers is still stuck on gcc 4.7.2 which has
incomplete support for uniform initialization. This was causing it to
error when surrounding a value with {} to cause a copy. Instead don't
surround the value with {} as it will be copied anyway.

Clear out a junk file

WSL is weird.

Fix troves randbooks of necromancy (PureQuestion)

At some point, we really should do some pass on the slightly different
phrasings between skill names, short-form spell school names, enhancer
artifact property names, staff names, and manual names, or make them more
easily accepted each others' specific terms, on the C side of Crawl's
set-up here. These many weird slight differences between the syntax for
each set up by different people is highly error prone at best, and it
would reasonably follow up with better vault error handling done in
02af3d5 and a6e618e.

Still, for now, this should once more make troves properly place
randbooks when one's highest school is Necromancy, which was broken in the
name tweaks of ad89b66, with yet another argument in the table of
generating hyper-specific magical items for both troves and one's own
apts / species / mutations.

Let Qazlal's Elemental Force convert blastmotes and fluffiness

The former can technically be set up in advance, but produces the least
impactful of the four elementals for a lot of MP. The latter is currently
only for cute interactions with the dreamshard necklace or the Cloud Mage's
Chambers, with the latter not being that threatening to Qazlal worshippers
in the first place due to their cloud immunity. Still, it makes fairly more
thematic sense with smoke clouds into air elementals in the first place,
and it can cover for a future planned use of the cloud tying into that
necklace.

Some more rltiles/UNUSED tidying

This clears out a few tiles redundant with tile additions and reuses, and
adds a fistful of old tiles from roctavian's submissions back in the day.
I've got a pretty large repository of this stuff myself, but I'm still a
little uncertain as to how much this folder is useful for other devs trying
to grab oldtiles as placeholders, so this is still a very small portion of
such.

Fix more cases of spells aiming at targets they can't hit

If you tried casting a spell with no tageter at a monster you can't hit
or at a spot near one, going back into the spell targeter would default
to aiming at the monster you can't hit.

Fix some spells aiming at monsters they can't hit

Following b0ab3d4, spells with no targeter (e.g. haunt) would sometimes
aim at monsters they can't hit, because we now consider targets even if
they are blocked by transparent obstructions.

Fix due_index_antique_fridge placing a "long blade" (Ogregutan)

It was presumably meant to place a "long sword" as a "long blade" isn't
a valid item.

Fix monsters dying to barbs sometimes crashing

If a monster died from barbs when it moved. It would have its position
set to (0, 0). We would then calculate its old position based on its
current position and how it had moved (e.g. if it had moved right one
square we would calculate its old position as (-1, 0)). We would then
pass this old position to monster::check_redraw which would crash if it
was out of the map bounds. Instead, only run the movement effects after
calling monster::check_redraw so that the monster doesn't die to barb
damage until after it has been redrawn.

Remove old unused domino code

When dominoes were reworked in b716198, some of the old code was left
behind even though it isn't used anymore.

Fix Gyre and Gimbal not getting bonus reckless damage

Gyre and Gimbal is a two handed weapon so it should get bonus damage
from the reckless mutation. However, we were accidentally only using its
type (quick blade) which is one-handed to determine if it should get
bonus damage.

Fix different races getting different unrands in seeded game (khaemere)

When upgrading a weapon into an unrand, we only consider unrand weapons
that use the same attack skill and number of hands as the weapon we are
upgrading. However, different races differ in the number of hands they
need to use a weapon (e.g. formicids use almost all weapons in one hand)
sometimes resulting in us picking a different unrand.

To fix this, require that the unrand uses the same number of hands for a
medium size player with no mutations when upgrading weapons for
non-acquirement items.

Resolves #5119

Give sewage sovereigns rPois (knorpule3001)

Every monster placing only in the Gulch is meant to have rPois to connect
to surviving its awful polluted flavour, even including living monsters
that themselves don't use poison like scrapshell chimeras and
telencephalons. Sewage sovereigns missing out on it is more of an oversight
than anything else meant to tweak balance with one way or another.
remove a duplicate credits entry
update credits.txt

Prevent mirroring minmay_shattered_shrine (#5234)

The minmay_shattered_shrine vault contains a QR-code-style layout. QR
codes remain readable when rotated, but horizontal or vertical mirroring
breaks the encoded pattern.

Add no_hmirror and no_vmirror to preserve the intended layout while
still allowing normal rotation.

commit.message:
Fix Xom trying to teleport those with stasis

When the Xom action-picking code was rewritten back in 6874458, it
accidentally introduced Xom trying to teleport formicids (and -Tele
item wearers) on only one of two effects due to using an `or` instead
of an `and`. It is a reasonably funny accidental joke in a certain
capacity for Xom to do this and fail, but it has reasonably run its
course and affects the hostile actions Xom will actually do on a Formicid,
and would probably work better as another line in Xom's endless flavour
messages list instead.

Report vault testing weights to players

There's been a fair number of these errors over the long course of Crawl's
history, and as with 02af3d5, I would rather make potential errors be
more easily caught and addressed quickly over such quietly lurking in the
background indefinitely. This commit makes Crawl report in just the
message log whenever a vault itself has more than 5000 weight (and not any
monsters or items used within it)- after two decades, we've got a single
decorative vault with 1500 weight and another four with >100 <1000
alongside a few dummy vault weight balancers, so it's very unlikely any
new vault weight assignments will deliberately go over this for anything
meant to be actually pushed to master.

Various other portal tweaks

Worthy of note:

 * While the rest of the bailey killstat curve since 4e6b4df has reached a
   reasonable gradient, bailey_axe_4 is still underperforming a fair bit.
   As a portal layout emphasizing both water and traps, I would prefer to
   emphasize that more than increasing the enemy density compared to most
   other portals, so aside from another 10% chance to place an orc knight,
   the majority of changes here emphasize more traps and less early space
   away from the river.

 * hellmonk_volcano_collapse being so fast with its wall-raising gimmick
   and ambushing immediately with all monsters has made it the second
   most lethal volcano layout in killstats after adjusting for map
   weights, which isn't inherently unreasonable but is fairly mean for a
   map with a unique spoilery gimmick. I'm increasing the minimum timer
   for walls to fall down onto the map and marking the spaces where walls
   appear both, which should allow actually rushing forward to get any of
   the loot without necessarily locking yourself in nearly immediately
   as well as reducing spoiler capacities after the first time one sees
   the layout. I'm also reducing the outside map boss count's cap to
   space them out a little, allowing the choice of leaving to be less
   reliant on noise rng.

 * The dragon trove of Erebora now properly spawns its randbook of
   Dragon's Call alongside the other elemental books.

 * Cigotuvi's Wizlab has gotten another actual threat buff to correlate
   with its high reward count and low kill rate: denser groupings and a
   chance for higher hd on the randomized glowing shapeshifter
   distribution, a perm_ench:chaos_laced buff on Cigotuvi's Monster to
   help it stand out yet more, and a small chance for kobold fleshcrafters
   accompanying occasionally-stronger ugly things. In exchange, it has
   gotten further talisman choice tweaks (as still have a fair number of
   character-clashing hits).

Always display petrifying status (Monkooky)

I think this combination suppression stuff should be removed altogether, but
let's start with this one since displaying both petrifying and paralysis is
clearly useful.

Fixes #4723

Yet further Xom boredom adjustments

These are somewhat stopgap measures while the project to finally fully
revamp Xom's strategic effects (mentioned in c7f2f74) has ended up
rising fairly higher on the project priority list. While the intensity
of this scaling will still be tuned down substantially when that project
is completed, the overall conditions for Xom's boredom can still be
tweaked in fashions fine for Xom boredom existing in the first place.

 * Xom's boredom declarations are no longer uselessly randomized
   seperately from actually deincrementing Xom's boredom, which previously
   resulted in a ton of random interruptions by the 1/20 chance every turn
   to win a coinflip at near-bottom boredom (which got amplified by
   trying to warn at much higher boredom points on nearly completed
   floors, which itself was left overly high after the nerf on c7f2f74).
   This overemphasized the risk of Xom's actions even on unexplored
   floors, and was a very confusing and hostile means of displaying
   information to begin with- appropriate for Xom, but far less fun than
   the random hostile actions to begin with.

   Instead, Xom now explicitly only declares being bored when Their
   boredom timer ticks down its last few points (scaled to fully explored
   floors), with some obfuscating fuzzing of actually stating these
   messages before the very last few triggers to retain any chaotic
   flavour:

   | 6      | 5      | 4      | 3      | 2     | 1     |
   -----------------------------------------------------
   | 50.00% | 57.14% | 66.67% | 80.00% | 100%  | 100%  |

   These last two triggers are also distinguished by the player being
   described as "VERY BORING" rather than now just "Boring". Hopefully,
   this should involve far less interruptions to communicate far more
   usefully "actually do something".

 * When Xom acts on a nearly-fully-explored floor while bored, the timer
   reset is now only 75% lower compared to the previous 33%. Since the
   strategic benefits and strategic maluses are already so much worse on
   those floors to begin with, Xom doesn't really need to shove players
   deeper that much faster to have the same "stop backtracking and start
   doing things" push, and this should allow making it to the next floor
   without having to warp play with manual exploration.

 * Xom's bored nearly-fully-explored floor estimate no longer guarantees
   bad mutations (but still amplifies drain + doom and disables getting
   strategic gifts). Unlike the comparable downsides of drain and doom
   also amplified here, bad mutations are a much meaner roll that can't be
   reasonably worked off in regular non-scumming play, and with scumming
   still at a very unviable state from withholding the item / bazaar gifts
   on known floors, these anti-scumming measure don't need to be quite as
   hostile as prior.

Further Gulch adjustments

Some of this about helping parsing unique branch mechanics, some of this
is about an extraordinarily low kill rate of 1 death in 57 games in the
past week, and some of this is about adjusting the future chance and
reward structure of the portal entirely.

 * The transient mutation warning for entering Gulch now explicitly
   mentions doing so provides exactly three transient mutations, rather
   than what can be parsed as much less.

 * The mutation catalyst tiles no longer have plinths, which I had hoped
   would be read as akin to many altar designs do but which instead can
   be read as statues.

   * TODO: let standing on a feature not prevent examining it with xv.

 * There is now a coinflip chance in each Gulch layout to place some
   number of traps- mostly tyrant traps with rare dispersal or harlequin
   or Zot traps- around various corners. Alongside further leaning into
   unique terrain being emphasized here more than any other lategame
   portal, if this does indeed end up becoming a proper branch than
   these vaults are unlikely to get no_trap_gen compared to most other
   vaults anyway.

 * With that extraordinarily low killrate, I have bumped up the boss
   numbers- a 1/3 chance depending on the boss theme set for a third
   sewage sovereign, second scrapshell chimera, or a formless jellyfish
   accompanying the amoeba / slymdra roll. I'm reluctant to do much
   else on the difficulty front here with the rarity of the late portal
   providing a very poor sample size, but if this still provides poor
   numbers after increased spawns and these additional threats then I
   will focus on buffing individual bespoke monsters next.

 * Item rewards have been nudged up for basically the last form I feel
   particularly comfortable about with such poor killstats- potions
   consistently come in stacks of two, and the generic superb_item
   meant to provide loot variety is now handpicked to be stronger than
   the average superb_item result: a mix of the same top-tier weapons
   of venom / hand cannons as prior (which can be rebranded as always),
   swamp dragon scales randarts, thematic ego aux armour or jewellery
   randarts, rare thematic evokers or unthemed randbooks and manuals.

 * The Gulch appearance rate is also nudged up from ~18.5% of all V:1-4
   visits to ~21.9%. I'm quite reluctant to hand out yet more item
   rewards in lategame after buffing both troves and Crypt rewards _and_
   _and_ the portal hasn't provided almost any threat to kill people
   with (compare the restrained rewards in Necropolis layouts due to the
   extremely controllable nature of the portal, Wizlabs having very poor
   non-magic rewards on average, Desolation being wildly dangerous in
   exchange for high rewards, Bazaars costing money). As long as the
   catalyst is actually noticed, the portal should continue to foreground
   themed and unique rewards, at least.

   * There's also not nearly as many layouts as I'd like for making
     the portal substantially more common yet, with further layouts
     waiting on submissions or other major projects this version to be
     finished. There could be a set-up to get one of Desolation or Gulch
     each game to deal with both layout and reward concerns here, though
     I'd like the difficulty of thie portal to be more settled first.

Fix Gauntlet end-subvault wall graphics issues (Odds)

This is a follow-up to 73e2906, fixing how gauntlet_exit_green and
gauntlet_exit_hidden_loot would map walls (and just walls) next to their
backmost outer floor, by adjusting the automap handling code (and leaving
a comment on where it's used). It's a little janky, but as long as we're
only using this no_automap tag for Gauntlets, it should be fine.

New decoration + yet further and further vault review

This commit mostly introduces assortments of trash, another decorative
floor reskinning option usable in a variety of generic lived-in vaults,
as a means of reusing the flavour and graphics of some of Gulch elsewhere
without having that individual vault match the portal's green floor tiles.
(In particular, the agnostic nature of these further encroaches upon some
plainer abstract vaults, which would be ideal to keep freshening up-
while it may take indefinite amounts of new tiles to really make the
average vault feel unique, it's a decent enough goal that each bit helping
for indefinite future vault development feels worthwhile.) Such pieces
combine the previous use of Sastreii's floor_studio papers and various CC0
tiny jrpg item graphics by finalbossblues / ARoachIFoundOnMyPillow / Jetrel
with more CC0 item graphics by RavenTale.

(The rest of this is mostly mild incidental error fix-ups and a tweak of
another custom_books book.)

Fix the message when a contam explosion ends fear (croikle)

When a fleeing monster was hit by a magical contamination explosion, it
would give a message such as "INVALID YOU_FAULTLESS attack snaps the
shining eye out of its fear", instead, say "The attack snaps the shining
eye out of its fear".

Fixes #5232

Fix AC for switching armour being incorrect when in a form

While in a form that melds armour, if you examined some armour that you
weren't wearing it would tell you that switching to the armour would
increase your AC even though you couldn't benefit from it. It would even
say your AC would increase when your current armour was better. This is
because it would count your current armour as melded but wouldn't count
the armour you were examining as melded when working out what AC you
would have when wearing it.

Fixes #5096

Speed up tile variant picking

Tile variant picking was spending most of its time handling dominoes.
However, those are only used when creating Crypt levels and aren't
needed when picking a tile variant for every terrain tile on screen
every frame. Instead move domino handling out of tile variant picking
and only call it when creating levels.
Remove duplicate slime stone wall tile variation

Don't allow knockback to take monsters out of bounds

This could cause rare crashes with wall walkers being trampled

Improve vault itemspec error handling, fix found errors (DracoOmega)

Crawl's item specification and definition error handling has had
extremely significant errors in it since 0.16. The way it parses items in
a list will silently pass by incorrect entries as long as there is another
valid item further in that list (since the error message it would report
gets replaced by the next work not having any errors). While failing
silently and gracefully allows vaults with some working definitions to
still place the working part of an item list, it's very noticeably against
the policy of Crawl not loading from other significant vault errors, and
it is exceedingly bad policy to completely hide failing item entries from
anybody working on Crawl for relatively indefinite periods of development.

This commit, with some assistance from DracoOmega, makes Crawl print
errors instead of loading whenever it actually parses that an incorrect
item has been passed to vault creation in a vault. I have also thus
gone through and fixed every incorrect item specification Crawl reports:
for the most part, this only fixes minor errors, but most notably it
reintroduces small chances of getting multiple ally scrolls in each Trove
post-rework and also catches the ziggurat potion of mutation syntax error
introduced in c79536f.

(Our limited means of error handling means that, unfortunately, we can't
have Crawl cleanly present every vault's syntax errors at once. It also
means we can't really try to, say, have Crawl report these issues to users
and then load in other valid item specifications or other vaults entirely
without stopping play dead. Further adjustments here may be beneficial,
but for now, this falling into the same means of handling as other .des
syntax errors is fairly more consistent and helps catch more actual
errors.)

Tweak some descriptions

 * Metal walls no longer mention they're bluish-grey- quite a few vaults
   recolour them as is, and the default metal wall tiles that the vast
   majority of players have seen for the past decade are not blue in any
   fashion.

 * Fungus in Gulch are now described as mauve instead of grey, which
   should be usefully ambigious enough to match both types of fungus
   tile colour there while being substantially more accurate than calling
   them "grey".

 * Athame now are described as knives rather than daggers, to reduce the
   chance of misinterpretting them as getting the dagger-exclusive extra
   stabbing damage bonus, and as newer weapons people may otherwise
   misinterpret.

Give sewage sovereigns immunity to Porkalator (tekkud)

The odds of dragging a bound Kirke far enough to see this were
incredulously unlikely to come up in a thousand games, but it is
consistent with the Porkalator immunity hell hogs already have, so it might
as well exist.

Buff Gulch item rewards + add a unique new reward feature (roxie puddles)

 * Gulch layouts now feature three more superb_items and one more randart
   talisman, to offer more mixed potential reward benefits still heavily
   balanced out by the high threat levels of the portal and the usual
   blind balancing capacity for rewards to match a specific character's
   needs.

 * The thematic Gulch rewards have been nudged up a little bit, with an
   increased (if not very high) chance of randarts using the flavour
   artprops for the plainer egos in the armour slot, and orb randarts
   specifying a few valuable types of orb to remove the chance of rolling
   e.g. orbs of mayhem as mostly just exist for ziggurat delving.

 * Most notably, there is now a completely new and distinct feature added
   to parallel the transient mutation entry penalty as well as provide
   another distinct reward from other portals. In the boss chambers of
   each Gulch is a single "purified mutation catalyst", which upon being
   interacted with (via standing on it and pressing `>`, as has been
   established with altars and shops both) gives a player a single good
   mutation before breaking apart. This leaves the decorative floor
   feature of an empty mutation catalyst, and a small number of Gulch
   layouts contain such decorative features alongside the usable one.

   * This mutation source can never roll the three flat stat increase
     mutations for being very boring (c.f. 67c6cfe), nor any body slot
     auxes as can have the notable downside of disabling equipment slots
     (most pressingly, including horns and antennae dropping helmets
     with one level). Zin worshippers and undead still can't mutate,
     though those in Death Form can access these catalysts if they
     transform back to normal.

   * These are deliberately features rather than e.g. returning potions of
     beneficial mutation to amplify the interests and pressures of
     mutation roulette- if a player wants more than just a single good
     mutation for their character, they have to actually commit to
     drinking potions of mutation brought in prior. (The flashiness of
     the reward as distinct from any other portal, and establishing
     that portals can affect characters beyond just item rewards, and
     preventing item hoarding with our expanded inventory size limits,
     all also contribute to this particular design decision.)

   * Currently, autoexplore will stop on seeing the catalyst, but not
     automatically travel towards or take it due to how one may thus
     want to drink potions of mutation first. Additionally, there is
     no prompt for using it. This might warrant some adjustment to stop
     ontop of it without actually using it automatically, then bringing
     in the usual yes / no prompt, but this is otherwise following some
     of the precedents set by altars.

   * The mutation catalyst tile in both usable and used-up states
     combines CanOfWorms's old Pakellas altar with a CC0 potion graphic
     by idylwild.

Another quick and brief vault review (+ a minor Zig change)

Worthy of note:

 * pf_dread_powers_of_hell has constantly gotten confused reports of it
   deploying reapers and balrugs not wielding their iconic weapons, as
   both of them haven't kept the wands of flame they were supposed to
   be given by the vault for multiple years. Rather than revert it to
   the state prior to a1918b6 and end up using crimson imps in Depths,
   I am opting to remove the monsters from the vault entirely, which is
   a mild shame but fairly less confusing.

 * A few Gulch monsters have been added to the usual hyper-randomized
   Sprint trio (arenasprint, zigsprint, and pitsprint) as are closest to
   contemporary design principles.

 * I've made another new Arena map (the testing one, not the Oka one)
   with 2-tile-wide corridors to test performances beyond open spaces
   or 1-tile-wide corridors.

 * hellmonk_guarded_unrand_damnation no longer uses hellions in Elf,
   partially because I've got mixed feelings about directly deploying
   demons in the branch with such diminished demonology themes, and
   partially so one of the best ranged weapon unrands can actually be
   guarded by one of the most dangerous ranged monster in the game.

 * Ziggurats past the first one cleared redistribute the weight of potions
   of experience over to potions of mutation, as the way potions of
   experience are currently implemented makes them give very little
   besides the experience level by the time one enters a zig, and
   rerolling mutations still holds some interest for megazig divers.

Nerf early D generic draugr once more

Most non-specified derived undead placements get extra effective depth for
the monster they pick, due to derived undead mostly missing out on many
of the strengths of the monster they take. This was excluded from draugr in
98bede3 due to them having a fairly reasonable trade-off of AC and an
AF_DOOM weapon hit, but was accidentally renabled when 76c8eab set up V
draugr as being stronger for their depth. While most early draugr placement
specifies monster types to prevent them from being too mean, a few overflow
altars and serial_undead don't, which can do with tuning down for
consistency's sake since so many draugr are quite difficult to even damage
at all early on.

As such, this commit once more removes the derived undead extra floor
scaling for generic draugr placement. (This doesn't apply to Crypt, though,
which already needs as much spice as it can get for its overlap with the
rest of lategame).

Explicitly mention shields can't block penetrating projectiles

This adjusts the Shields skill description, javelins, and several bolt
spell descriptions to hopefully better communicate to newcomers that if a
projectile can go entirely through three orcs in plate armour before it
hits a target, a shield isn't going to block such either.

Make manticores count as winged quadrupeds (tekkud)

In almost any medieval bestiary manuscript collection, over 90% of
manticores don't actually have wings. This common perception is, as usual,
mostly D&D's fault, and can plausibly be traced back to Dante insisting
Geryon was a human-faced stinger-bearing dragon-lion for the sake of a
winged mount planar crawl. Unfortunately, Crawl needs manticores to be able
to fly so Shoals has more lowest-level fodder, and I'm pretty doubtful
people would entirely take wingless manticores swimming through the Shoals
water that seriously, so I'm updating their bodyplan to match their
capacity to fly and their tile both.

(This mostly just affects manticore derived undead tile choices, for the
record, which got extra highlighted with the additional spectral tiles
added in 70a134b.)

Make LRD hit plasmodia (#5231)

LRD cast directly at a creeping plasmodium was not hurting it. This was
because the logic for LRD explosions was "don't affect the centre unless
exploding terrain with a non-solid tile".

We change the logic to "don't affect the centre when exploding a
monster", which should be correct in all cases.
